---
title: "From Genomic Data to Clinical Insight"
---

# From Genomic Data to Clinical Insight

This project outlines how I approach the design of a reproducible analysis workflow that connects genomic data processing, statistical analysis, and interpretable reporting.

## Workflow overview

Raw Data → Quality Control → Processing → Statistical Analysis → Interpretation → Reporting

## Why this matters

Clinical and research workflows need more than analysis alone. They also require data validation, reproducibility, and outputs that can be understood by collaborators with different backgrounds.

## Key components

### 1. Quality control
Checking data quality, missingness, and consistency before downstream analysis.

### 2. Structured analysis
Using statistical methods appropriate to the study design and research question.

### 3. Interpretation
Placing findings in context, including limitations and practical relevance.

### 4. Reporting
Presenting outputs in a form that supports communication, review, and reuse.
